surface.name: OpenAI Responses API via cURL (API key + funded credits)
surface.selection_basis: OpenAI's Developer quickstart creates an API key and demonstrates a Responses API call. The same page documents a cURL request to https://api.openai.com/v1/responses with Authorization: Bearer $OPENAI_API_KEY, so an SDK install is not required. OpenAI prepaid-billing help states that after purchasing credits you can start using the API, and billing setup includes adding payment details.

alternatives_considered.name: JavaScript / Python / other SDK tabs
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: SDK tabs are optional convenience clients. The quickstart also documents cURL against the same Responses endpoint with only an API key.

alternatives_considered.name: Agents SDK quickstart
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This is a later specialized agent path after a basic Responses API call.

alternatives_considered.name: Chat Completions legacy path
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The current Developer quickstart centers the Responses API as the first call.

entry_point.developer_intent: Create an OpenAI Platform API key, fund usable prepaid credits with a payment method, and receive a Responses API JSON result over HTTPS.
assumed_prior_state: The developer has no OpenAI Platform API key, prepaid credit balance, or prior Responses request.
A terminal with cURL is available; no language SDK is assumed.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: Run an API Call to the Responses API
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: An authenticated HTTPS Responses API request to https://api.openai.com/v1/responses returns model-generated output text.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The Responses API returns a successful JSON body that includes model output text for the requested prompt.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: The Developer quickstart documents Create an API Key, export OPENAI_API_KEY, and a cURL POST to https://api.openai.com/v1/responses with Authorization: Bearer $OPENAI_API_KEY. Prepaid billing help states that after purchasing credits you should be able to start using the API.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: The first meaningful LLM success is a successful authenticated Responses API response. An SDK is not required because the official quickstart documents the same call with cURL. Usable prepaid credits (and payment details to purchase them when free-trial credit is $0) are required for API usage per OpenAI's prepaid billing help; a key alone is not enough once free-trial credit is exhausted or unavailable.

prerequisites.requirement: An OpenAI Platform account that can open the API keys and billing settings.

Payment details and prepaid credits with a usable balance. OpenAI's prepaid billing setup shows Add payment details and states that after purchasing credits you can start using the API; exhausted credits return a billing-quota error on API requests.
An API key created in the OpenAI dashboard and stored securely.
A terminal with cURL (or any HTTP client) able to send HTTPS JSON requests. No language SDK is required.

primary_path.action: Create or sign in to an OpenAI Platform account and open the dashboard.
primary_path.input: Intent to use the OpenAI API
primary_path.success_signal: API keys and billing settings are reachable.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Account recovery follows the dashboard sign-in flow.

primary_path.action: Open billing settings, add payment details, and purchase prepaid credits (or confirm a usable credit balance).
details: Prepaid billing setup includes Add payment details under the billing overview.
After purchasing credits, OpenAI states you should be able to start using the API; systems may take a couple of minutes to reflect the balance.
If free-trial credit remaining is already $0, payment details and a credit purchase are required before API usage continues.
primary_path.success_signal: Billing shows a positive usable credit balance (or non-zero free-trial credit still available).
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Payment verification delays or a $0 balance leave API requests failing with billing-quota errors.

primary_path.action: Create an API key in the OpenAI dashboard and store it securely.
details: The quickstart warns to keep the key in a safe location.
primary_path.success_signal: The key is generated and copied to secure local storage.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: No manual approval wait is documented for key creation.

primary_path.action: Export the key as OPENAI_API_KEY in the terminal.
details: macOS/Linux: export OPENAI_API_KEY=...; PowerShell: setx OPENAI_API_KEY ...
primary_path.output: API key available to HTTP clients in the shell
primary_path.success_signal: The environment variable is set for the current shell.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Shell persistence depends on the OS.

primary_path.action: POST a Responses API request with cURL to https://api.openai.com/v1/responses.
details: Include Content-Type: application/json and Authorization: Bearer $OPENAI_API_KEY.
Send a JSON body with a model and input prompt as shown in the quickstart cURL example.
primary_path.success_signal: The API returns a successful JSON body containing model output text.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Missing key, insufficient credits, or invalid JSON produce API errors instead of model output.

primary_path.action: Confirm the Responses API JSON includes the model-generated text.
primary_path.success_signal: Model output text is present in the successful response.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: An error body means the first call did not succeed.

branches.condition: The account still has usable free-trial credit remaining.
branches.path: Skip purchasing additional prepaid credits while free-trial credit remains; the quickstart may still show Congrats on running a free test API request. When free-trial credit is $0, add payment details and purchase credits before further API usage.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Billing purchase may be deferred only while free-trial credit remains usable.

branches.condition: The developer prefers an official SDK instead of cURL.
branches.path: Install the language SDK shown on the quickstart tab and run the equivalent Responses example; the API endpoint and API-key header requirements stay the same.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds optional local package installation without changing the authenticated Responses boundary.

friction_gates.description: Payment details and prepaid credits are required to start or continue API usage when free-trial credit is $0; exhausted credits cause billing-quota API errors.
A secret API key must be created and stored safely.
The Authorization Bearer token must be the API key for https://api.openai.com/v1/responses.

sources.title: Developer quickstart
sections_used: Create and export an API key
Congrats on running a free test API request
evidence_supported: cURL POST to /v1/responses with Bearer token
Free-test congratulations and post-call Add credits CTA

sources.title: How can I set up prepaid billing?
evidence_supported: Add payment details in billing overview
Purchase prepaid credits before using the API
Billing-quota errors when credits are exhausted
Possible short delay after credit purchase

uncertainties.question: Which brand-new accounts currently receive non-zero free-trial credit versus $0 free-trial credit requiring immediate payment details?
uncertainties.impact: Some accounts may complete the first Responses call before purchasing credits; others hit billing before any successful LLM response.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The quickstart mentions a free test request while prepaid billing help shows Free trial credit remaining $0.00 with Add payment details; eligibility is account-state dependent and not fully enumerated on these pages.

excluded_after_success: Higher rate limits beyond initial prepaid balance
